learning_outcomes.description: Implement object-oriented programs according to their specifications.
Design, implement and document object-oriented programs using language features such as inheritance, interfaces, polymorphism, exceptions and I/O.
Design and implement test components for object-oriented programs.
Analyse code to judge whether it follows good programming practice.
Write, interpret and critique specifications for program modules (e.g. classes or interfaces).

assessments.category: Computer Code, Quiz, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessments.task_description:
Every week, starting in Week 1, you will be given sets of simple practical Java programming problems to solve during the semester. These problem sets will be delivered and submitted via the Ed Lessons online environment. Each set of problems will reinforce concepts delivered in the course. Refer to the assessment description on Blackboard.

A link to this environment is available on Blackboard.

The due date and time will be before 5:00pm the following Thursday of each week, commencing with the first set (Week 1) due before 5:00pm on the Thursday of Week 2; and the last set (Week 12) due before 5:00pm on the Thursday of Week 13.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task.
assessments.submission_guidelines: These problem sets will be delivered and submitted via the Ed Lessons online environment. A link to this environment is available on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Assignments are used to assess the practical skills of students on non-trivial, but reasonably well-defined problems.

AI tools are used as part of normal work or professional practices in this course. Considerations for AI use is part of assessed work. Students are expected to consider AI in an appropriate way, to interpret and check any AI-generated outputs, and to take responsibility for the quality and correctness of their work. Students are responsible for ensuring that any use of AI complies with academic integrity requirements and is appropriately acknowledged. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students may be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their submission independent of AI and MT tools, as part of the assignment marking process.

All submitted code will be subject to electronic code design and structure plagiarism and collusion detection. The assignments are to be worked on individually and must be your own work except where the use of code written or provided by other entities (teaching staff, AI tools, etc.) is explicitly permitted by the assignment specification, and any such code is referenced in the manner required in the assignment specification.

A portion of the Assignment 1 marking and feedback is immediate on every submission and every re-submission, giving students instantaneous feedback on more than a quarter of the overall Assignment 1 assessment. Note that the due date for Assignment 1 is before the Census date. This gives students submitting by the due date the opportunity to receive immediate feedback before the Census date.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online via Gradescope unless otherwise specified for a particular assessment item. Details will be provided with the assignment specification.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The questions on the exam will be similar in format to previous semesters and will cover all course content. You may use past exams as practice questions. The final exam (including any deferred or supplementary exams) are closed-book examinations and no student notes of any kind are permitted in the examination room. Any general notes that will provided with the examination papers will be also made available to students before the start of the revision period.

As the final exam (including any deferred or supplementary exams) are in-person technology-free paper-based secure exams, Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are not available to students while performing this assessment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
